summary: Project DNA is an AI-powered CLI tool that preserves software project architectural and business knowledge in a local, structured format to enable context-aware AI coding assistants and reduce hallucinations.

framework.name: Node.js CLI
evidence: Overview states it operates as a CLI application

architectureStyle.reasoning: Operates as a local CLI tool with a modular structure separating scanning, AI reasoning, and local persistence. Designed to be provider-agnostic for AI reasoning.

layers.name: CLI Interface
layers.description: Handles command parsing, user interaction, and terminal UI

layers.name: Core Logic
layers.description: Scanning, AI reasoning orchestration, and local persistence

modules.name: Scanner
modules.responsibility: Analyzes repository structure, dependencies, and configuration

modules.name: AI Engine
modules.responsibility: Interfaces with LLM providers to reason about architecture

boundaries.rule: CLI commands trigger core logic workflows
Validated AI output is persisted locally

businessDomains.name: Architectural Governance
businessDomains.description: Capturing and preserving architectural decisions, context, and engineering standards.

businessDomains.name: Knowledge Management
businessDomains.description: Storing and organizing project technical and business knowledge in a structured format.

businessDomains.name: AI Reasoning
businessDomains.description: Analyzing project data to infer architectural insights, business intent, and domain context.

technicalDomains.name: CLI
technicalDomains.description: Command-line interface for user interaction and command execution.

technicalDomains.name: AI Integration
technicalDomains.description: Provider abstraction for LLM reasoning and prompt execution.

technicalDomains.name: Validation
technicalDomains.description: Schema validation for structured AI output using Zod.

dependencyIntent.reasoning: Dependencies directly support CLI operations, AI provider integration, schema validation, and file system manipulation.

businessIntent.overview: Project DNA is an AI-powered architectural governance platform that preserves the technical and business knowledge of software projects. It operates as a CLI tool that scans repositories and uses AI to generate a persistent, local knowledge base, enabling AI coding assistants to understand the project deeply.
goals: Preserve architectural context and business intent.
Reduce AI hallucinations and architectural inconsistencies.
Provide a persistent knowledge layer for AI interactions.
Maintain a local-first, version-controllable architectural memory.
businessIntent.coreValue: Serving as the architectural memory of software projects, enabling AI assistants to generate accurate, context-aware, and architecturally consistent solutions.

patterns: Prefer TypeScript for new implementation work.
architectureRules: Keep architecture knowledge centralized in .pdna files.
AI layer must be decoupled from specific LLM providers.
AI must not generate source code, only architectural knowledge.

securityConcerns.title: Secret Management
securityConcerns.description: Avoid storing secrets in source files.

securityConcerns.title: Sensitive Context Exposure
securityConcerns.description: Treat architecture knowledge as sensitive project context.

riskAreas.reason: While provider abstraction is planned, current implementation relies on specific provider SDKs.
Scanner detected React, but dependencies indicate a Node.js CLI tool.

missingContext.reason: Scanner only provides 'src' as a source directory without detailed file breakdown.
Overview mentions AI reasoning but does not detail the prompt engineering strategies.

recommendedConstraints: Must not generate code, only architectural intelligence.
Must store all generated knowledge locally inside the project.
Must validate all AI outputs against predefined schemas before persistence.

importantModules.name: Scanner
importantModules.reason: Extracts technical reality from the repository.

importantModules.name: AI Engine
importantModules.reason: Transforms raw data and business overview into structured intelligence.

importantModules.name: Persistence
importantModules.reason: Ensures knowledge is stored locally for future AI consumption.

reasoningSummary: Merged scanner facts (TypeScript, CLI dependencies) with the human overview (AI governance, local-first architecture) to deduce a modular CLI architecture. Identified a discrepancy where the scanner detected React, but evidence strongly points to a Node.js CLI tool.

architecturalRecommendations.title: Enforce Provider Abstraction
architecturalRecommendations.description: Ensure all LLM interactions go through a generic provider interface.
architecturalRecommendations.rationale: Overview explicitly states the need to support multiple providers without affecting the core application.

architecturalRecommendations.title: Strict Schema Validation
architecturalRecommendations.description: Validate all AI outputs using Zod before writing to the .pdna directory.
architecturalRecommendations.rationale: Guarantees consistency, predictability, and compatibility across future versions.

notes: Scanner detected React with medium confidence, but dependencies and overview strongly indicate a Node.js CLI tool.
Overview provides clear business and architectural intent, reducing uncertainty in domain mapping.

scannerSignals: Dependencies include commander, chalk, ora, openai, zod
overviewSignals: Local-first architecture storing data in .pdna directory
Provider abstraction is a core principle
derivedSignals: Architecture is a modular monolith CLI tool
Zod is used for schema validation of AI outputs
fs-extra and execa are used for repository scanning
